---
title: 'Cache Adapters'
description: 'Storage adapters for Seyfert cache system'
---

## Overview

Cache adapters define how Seyfert stores and retrieves cached data. Seyfert provides built-in adapters for in-memory storage, and you can create custom adapters for databases like Redis, PostgreSQL, or any other storage backend.

## Adapter Interface

All adapters must implement the `Adapter` interface:

```typescript
interface Adapter {
  isAsync: boolean;

  start(): Awaitable<void>;
  
  scan(query: string, keys?: boolean): Awaitable<any[] | string[]>;
  
  bulkGet(keys: string[]): Awaitable<any[]>;
  get(keys: string): Awaitable<any | null>;
  
  bulkSet(keyValue: [string, any][]): Awaitable<void>;
  set(id: string, data: any): Awaitable<void>;
  
  bulkPatch(keyValue: [string, any][]): Awaitable<void>;
  patch(id: string, data: any): Awaitable<void>;
  
  values(to: string): Awaitable<any[]>;
  keys(to: string): Awaitable<string[]>;
  count(to: string): Awaitable<number>;
  
  bulkRemove(keys: string[]): Awaitable<void>;
  remove(keys: string): Awaitable<void>;
  
  flush(): Awaitable<void>;
  
  contains(to: string, keys: string): Awaitable<boolean>;
  
  getToRelationship(to: string): Awaitable<string[]>;
  bulkAddToRelationShip(data: Record<string, string[]>): Awaitable<void>;
  addToRelationship(to: string, keys: string | string[]): Awaitable<void>;
  removeToRelationship(to: string, keys: string | string[]): Awaitable<void>;
  removeRelationship(to: string | string[]): Awaitable<void>;
}
```

### Property

<ParamField path="isAsync" type="boolean" required>
  Indicates whether the adapter uses async operations. Set to `true` for database adapters, `false` for in-memory adapters.
</ParamField>

### Required Methods

<ParamField path="start" type="() => Awaitable<void>" required>
  Initialize the adapter. Called when the client starts.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="get" type="(key: string) => Awaitable<any | null>" required>
  Retrieve a single value by key. Returns `null` if not found.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="bulkGet" type="(keys: string[]) => Awaitable<any[]>" required>
  Retrieve multiple values by keys. Returns array of values (excluding nulls).
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="set" type="(id: string, data: any) => Awaitable<void>" required>
  Store a single value with the given key.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="bulkSet" type="(keyValue: [string, any][]) => Awaitable<void>" required>
  Store multiple key-value pairs at once.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="patch" type="(id: string, data: any) => Awaitable<void>" required>
  Update a value by merging with existing data. Arrays should replace, objects should merge.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="bulkPatch" type="(keyValue: [string, any][]) => Awaitable<void>" required>
  Update multiple values by merging with existing data.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="remove" type="(key: string) => Awaitable<void>" required>
  Remove a single entry by key.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="bulkRemove" type="(keys: string[]) => Awaitable<void>" required>
  Remove multiple entries by keys.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="flush" type="() => Awaitable<void>" required>
  Clear all data from storage.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="scan" type="(query: string, keys?: boolean) => Awaitable<any[] | string[]>" required>
  Scan for entries matching a pattern. Supports wildcards (`*`). If `keys` is true, return keys instead of values.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="values" type="(to: string) => Awaitable<any[]>" required>
  Get all values for a namespace.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="keys" type="(to: string) => Awaitable<string[]>" required>
  Get all keys for a namespace.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="count" type="(to: string) => Awaitable<number>" required>
  Count entries in a namespace.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="contains" type="(to: string, keys: string) => Awaitable<boolean>" required>
  Check if a key exists in a namespace.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="getToRelationship" type="(to: string) => Awaitable<string[]>" required>
  Get relationship data for a namespace. Used for tracking which IDs belong to which namespace.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="addToRelationship" type="(to: string, keys: string | string[]) => Awaitable<void>" required>
  Add keys to a namespace relationship.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="bulkAddToRelationShip" type="(data: Record<string, string[]>) => Awaitable<void>" required>
  Add multiple relationships at once.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="removeToRelationship" type="(to: string, keys: string | string[]) => Awaitable<void>" required>
  Remove keys from a namespace relationship.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="removeRelationship" type="(to: string | string[]) => Awaitable<void>" required>
  Remove entire namespace relationships.
</ParamField>

## Built-in Adapters

### MemoryAdapter

A simple in-memory storage adapter using JavaScript `Map`. Best for development and small bots.

```typescript
class MemoryAdapter<T> implements Adapter {
  isAsync = false;
  
  constructor(options?: MemoryAdapterOptions<T>);
}
```

#### Options

```typescript
interface MemoryAdapterOptions<T> {
  encode(data: any): T;
  decode(data: T): unknown;
}
```

<ParamField path="encode" type="(data: any) => T">
  Transform data before storing. Default: identity function.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="decode" type="(data: T) => unknown">
  Transform data when retrieving. Default: identity function.
</ParamField>

#### Example

```typescript
import { Client, MemoryAdapter } from 'seyfert';
import { GatewayIntentBits } from 'seyfert/lib/types';

const client = new Client({
  intents: GatewayIntentBits.Guilds,
  cache: {
    adapter: new MemoryAdapter(),
  },
});
```

#### Custom Encoding

```typescript
import { MemoryAdapter } from 'seyfert';

const adapter = new MemoryAdapter({
  // Compress data before storing
  encode(data) {
    return JSON.stringify(data);
  },
  // Decompress when retrieving
  decode(data) {
    return JSON.parse(data);
  },
});
```

### LimitedMemoryAdapter

An in-memory adapter with TTL (time-to-live) and size limits per resource type. Ideal for production bots that need memory management.

```typescript
class LimitedMemoryAdapter<T> implements Adapter {
  isAsync = false;
  
  constructor(options?: LimitedMemoryAdapterOptions<T>);
}
```

#### Options

```typescript
interface LimitedMemoryAdapterOptions<T> {
  default?: ResourceLimitedMemoryAdapter;
  
  guild?: ResourceLimitedMemoryAdapter;
  user?: ResourceLimitedMemoryAdapter;
  ban?: ResourceLimitedMemoryAdapter;
  member?: ResourceLimitedMemoryAdapter;
  voice_state?: ResourceLimitedMemoryAdapter;
  channel?: ResourceLimitedMemoryAdapter;
  emoji?: ResourceLimitedMemoryAdapter;
  presence?: ResourceLimitedMemoryAdapter;
  role?: ResourceLimitedMemoryAdapter;
  stage_instance?: ResourceLimitedMemoryAdapter;
  sticker?: ResourceLimitedMemoryAdapter;
  overwrite?: ResourceLimitedMemoryAdapter;
  message?: ResourceLimitedMemoryAdapter;
  
  encode?(data: any): T;
  decode?(data: T): unknown;
}

interface ResourceLimitedMemoryAdapter {
  expire?: number;  // TTL in milliseconds
  limit?: number;   // Max items to store
}
```

<ParamField path="default" type="ResourceLimitedMemoryAdapter">
  Default limits applied to all resources. Default: `{ limit: Infinity, expire: undefined }`
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="guild" type="ResourceLimitedMemoryAdapter">
  Limits specific to guilds
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="user" type="ResourceLimitedMemoryAdapter">
  Limits specific to users
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="member" type="ResourceLimitedMemoryAdapter">
  Limits specific to members
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="message" type="ResourceLimitedMemoryAdapter">
  Limits specific to messages
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="channel" type="ResourceLimitedMemoryAdapter">
  Limits specific to channels
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="encode" type="(data: any) => T">
  Transform data before storing
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="decode" type="(data: T) => unknown">
  Transform data when retrieving
</ParamField>

#### Example

```typescript
import { Client, LimitedMemoryAdapter } from 'seyfert';
import { GatewayIntentBits } from 'seyfert/lib/types';

const client = new Client({
  intents: GatewayIntentBits.Guilds | GatewayIntentBits.GuildMessages,
  cache: {
    adapter: new LimitedMemoryAdapter({
      // Default limits for all resources
      default: {
        limit: 1000,
        expire: 3600000, // 1 hour
      },
      // Messages: keep last 100 per channel, expire after 5 minutes
      message: {
        limit: 100,
        expire: 300000,
      },
      // Members: keep last 500 per guild, no expiration
      member: {
        limit: 500,
      },
      // Users: unlimited, expire after 1 hour
      user: {
        expire: 3600000,
      },
    }),
  },
});
```

## Creating Custom Adapters

You can create custom adapters for any storage backend. Here's an example Redis adapter:

### Redis Adapter Example

```typescript
import { Adapter } from 'seyfert';
import { createClient, type RedisClientType } from 'redis';

export class RedisAdapter implements Adapter {
  isAsync = true;
  private client: RedisClientType;
  
  constructor(redisUrl: string) {
    this.client = createClient({ url: redisUrl });
  }
  
  async start() {
    await this.client.connect();
  }
  
  async get(key: string) {
    const data = await this.client.get(key);
    return data ? JSON.parse(data) : null;
  }
  
  async bulkGet(keys: string[]) {
    const data = await this.client.mGet(keys);
    return data.filter(x => x).map(x => JSON.parse(x!));
  }
  
  async set(key: string, data: any) {
    await this.client.set(key, JSON.stringify(data));
  }
  
  async bulkSet(keyValue: [string, any][]) {
    const pipeline = this.client.multi();
    for (const [key, value] of keyValue) {
      pipeline.set(key, JSON.stringify(value));
    }
    await pipeline.exec();
  }
  
  async patch(key: string, data: any) {
    const existing = await this.get(key);
    const merged = Array.isArray(data)
      ? data
      : { ...existing, ...data };
    await this.set(key, merged);
  }
  
  async bulkPatch(keyValue: [string, any][]) {
    for (const [key, value] of keyValue) {
      await this.patch(key, value);
    }
  }
  
  async remove(key: string) {
    await this.client.del(key);
  }
  
  async bulkRemove(keys: string[]) {
    if (keys.length) await this.client.del(keys);
  }
  
  async flush() {
    await this.client.flushDb();
  }
  
  async scan(query: string, keys = false) {
    const pattern = query.replace(/\*/g, '*');
    const results: string[] = [];
    
    for await (const key of this.client.scanIterator({ MATCH: pattern })) {
      results.push(key);
    }
    
    if (keys) return results;
    return this.bulkGet(results);
  }
  
  async values(to: string) {
    const keys = await this.keys(to);
    return this.bulkGet(keys);
  }
  
  async keys(to: string) {
    const relationship = await this.getToRelationship(to);
    return relationship.map(id => `${to}.${id}`);
  }
  
  async count(to: string) {
    const relationship = await this.getToRelationship(to);
    return relationship.length;
  }
  
  async contains(to: string, key: string) {
    const isMember = await this.client.sIsMember(`rel:${to}`, key);
    return isMember;
  }
  
  async getToRelationship(to: string) {
    return this.client.sMembers(`rel:${to}`);
  }
  
  async addToRelationship(to: string, keys: string | string[]) {
    const keyArray = Array.isArray(keys) ? keys : [keys];
    if (keyArray.length) {
      await this.client.sAdd(`rel:${to}`, keyArray);
    }
  }
  
  async bulkAddToRelationShip(data: Record<string, string[]>) {
    const pipeline = this.client.multi();
    for (const [to, keys] of Object.entries(data)) {
      if (keys.length) pipeline.sAdd(`rel:${to}`, keys);
    }
    await pipeline.exec();
  }
  
  async removeToRelationship(to: string, keys: string | string[]) {
    const keyArray = Array.isArray(keys) ? keys : [keys];
    if (keyArray.length) {
      await this.client.sRem(`rel:${to}`, keyArray);
    }
  }
  
  async removeRelationship(to: string | string[]) {
    const keys = Array.isArray(to) ? to : [to];
    await this.client.del(keys.map(k => `rel:${k}`));
  }
}
```

### Using the Custom Adapter

```typescript
import { Client } from 'seyfert';
import { RedisAdapter } from './redis-adapter';
import { GatewayIntentBits } from 'seyfert/lib/types';

const client = new Client({
  intents: GatewayIntentBits.Guilds,
  cache: {
    adapter: new RedisAdapter('redis://localhost:6379'),
  },
});
```

## Testing Your Adapter

Seyfert provides a built-in test method to validate your adapter:

```typescript
import { Client } from 'seyfert';
import { MyCustomAdapter } from './my-adapter';

const client = new Client({
  cache: {
    adapter: new MyCustomAdapter(),
  },
});

// Test the adapter
await client.cache.testAdapter();
// If successful: "The adapter seems to work properly"
```

The test validates:
- Basic CRUD operations (create, read, update, delete)
- Bulk operations
- Relationship management
- Different resource types (BaseResource, GuildBasedResource, GuildRelatedResource)
- Count and flush operations

## Best Practices

<Tip>
  **Performance**: Implement bulk operations efficiently. Use pipelines or batch operations when available in your storage backend.
</Tip>

<Warning>
  **Relationships**: Properly implement relationship methods (`getToRelationship`, `addToRelationship`, etc.). These are critical for resource tracking and cleanup.
</Warning>

<Note>
  **Async vs Sync**: Set `isAsync` correctly. Seyfert uses this to determine whether to await operations.
</Note>

<Tip>
  **Error Handling**: Implement proper error handling in your adapter. Failed cache operations should not crash the bot.
</Tip>
